RED.action: Initiate controlled release protocol — regulate Idukki outflow to stay below 3,000 cumecs as per CWC circular CWC/DM/2018-19.
Notify downstream Periyar River monitoring stations at Aluva and Ernakulam of expected surge timing.
RED.source: Kerala State Disaster Management Authority SOP 2019

RED.action: Activate Emergency Action Plan (EAP) — Idukki Dam. Alert KSEB and State Disaster Management Control Room.

ORANGE.action: Monitor inflow rate at 30-minute intervals. Prepare controlled release if inflow exceeds 2,000 cumecs.

GREEN.action: Maintain standard monitoring schedule. Log water level readings every 6 hours.

RED.action: Deploy NDRF teams to Fort Kochi and Vyttila — pre-position rescue boats at Thevara Ferry Wharf and Marine Drive embankment.
RED.source: NDMA Standard Operating Procedures for Flood Response 2020

RED.action: Activate medical response units — coordinate with Ernakulam General Hospital and Amrita Institute for emergency triage.
RED.source: NDRF Field Manual, Chapter 7: Medical First Response

RED.action: Establish Incident Command Post at Willingdon Island — coordinate air and water rescue corridors.
RED.source: Kerala State Emergency Operations Centre Protocol 2018

ORANGE.action: Place NDRF teams on 2-hour standby. Conduct rapid vulnerability assessment of low-lying coastal wards.

GREEN.action: Maintain routine readiness. Update flood vulnerability maps for Fort Kochi and Vyttila wards.

RED.action: Issue Section 144 CrPC evacuation order for wards below 1m elevation — Fort Kochi, Mattancherry, Thevara. Activate all 47 flood relief camps.
Requisition KSRTC buses (minimum 50) for mass evacuation. Coordinate with Railway Division for special evacuation trains from Ernakulam South.
Declare District Disaster Alert — release emergency funds under SDRF. Notify State EOC, Revenue Commissioner, and Chief Minister Office.

ORANGE.action: Open pre-identified relief camps at Maharajas College and Ernakulam Town Hall. Alert village officers in flood-prone panchayats.

GREEN.action: Review ward-level flood vulnerability list. Confirm contact list for rapid alert dissemination.

RED.action: Immediately close NH66 between Vyttila and Aroor at water depth >0.3m. Deploy barricades and alternate route signage via Seaport-Airport Road.
Inspect and close Vyttila flyover if water level in Vembanad backwaters exceeds 0.8m MSL. Divert to Kundannoor bridge.
RED.source: PWD Kerala Emergency Road Closure Protocol

RED.action: Deploy JCBs and pumps at 5 critical underpasses — Palarivattom, Edapally, CSEZ, Kakkanad, Vytilla Hub.

ORANGE.action: Monitor NH66 water sensors every 30 minutes. Pre-position road closure equipment at Vyttila and Aroor junctions.

GREEN.action: Confirm functionality of all drain clearance systems on NH66 and SH1. Log pavement condition status.

RED.action: EVACUATE IMMEDIATELY if in Fort Kochi, Mattancherry, Thevara, or Vyttila. Move to nearest relief camp. Do NOT wait.
DO NOT attempt to cross flooded roads or underpasses. 15cm of fast-moving water can knock you off your feet. 30cm can sweep a vehicle.
Switch off electricity at mains before evacuating. Do not use lifts. Keep emergency numbers saved: DEOC 1077, NDRF 9446600421.

ORANGE.action: Pack 72-hour emergency kit (documents, medicines, water, food). Monitor DEOC alerts via CM Distress App and Akashavani FM 101.9 MHz.

GREEN.action: Stay informed. Download Kerala Disaster Management App. Know your nearest relief camp location.
